channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: States move to ban or restrict use of synthetic dyes used in candies and cereal
item.description: Synthetic dyes used to make brightly colored cereals, drinks and candies are coming under scrutiny in states across the country.
item.dc:creator: Leah Willingham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration struggled Wednesday to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, STEPHEN GROVES, and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: Democrats say the list originated from the desk of Peter Marocco, one of the leading figures dismantling USAID alongside Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging that his prosecution be moved out of New York state court so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court blocks Trump admin's deportation flights in Alien Enemies Act immigration suit
item.description: A federal appeals court issued a decision in a high-profile immigration case challenging the Trump administration's authority to deport Venezuelan nationals via a 1798 wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases the Signal chat showing Hegseth's detailed attack plans against the Houthis
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Atlantic on Wednesday released the entire Signal chat among senior national security officials, showing that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth provided the exact timings of warplane launches and when bombs would drop — before the men and women flying those attacks against Yemen’s
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans in Mike Waltz's old district aren't mad at him or Trump over Yemen Signal chat
item.description: DELAND, Fla. (AP) — As Washington was roiled by the news that one of President Donald Trump’s top aides added a journalist to a group chat discussing military plans, many Republicans going to the polls to replace that White House aide’s old U.S. House seat have brushed off the story.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s new executive order could upend voting
item.description: The order tests the power of Trump’s authority and would require voters using a federal form to show proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. It’s sure to be tested in court.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Walz announces remote work changes for Minnesota state agency employees
item.description: State agency employees will have to work in-person for at least 50 percent of scheduled workdays starting June 1. The policy change provides an exemption for employees who live more than 75 miles away from their primary work location.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Youngkin vetoes AI regulation legislation in Virginia
item.description: Youngkin said his administration and the state of Virginia already has established safeguards and oversight for AI use.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: The inside story of how a journalist was sent White House war plans
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor of The Atlantic, said he was mistakenly added to a group chat with U.S. national security leaders about imminent military strikes on Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan, Ailsa Chang, Christopher Intagliata, and Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Democrats call for hearing with Musk on DOGE operations, funding
item.description: The lawmakers are questioning what funds DOGE is using and if it should technically be considered a federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge contends Nazis got more due process than Trump deportees did
item.description: The fight over the rarely used wartime power has become central to Trump’s immigration crackdown agenda and his efforts to stretch the powers of the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News
